figures.caption: Fig. 1. Row 1: Comparison of Syn-to-Real generalization on ETH3D [2], Middlebury [3], KITTI-2012 [4], and Booster [5], where the lower metrics indicate better performance (Thick boundary methods use Vision-FoundationModel [6]). Row 2: Visual comparison with Selective-IGEV [7] on ETH3D. Row 3: Visual comparison with IGEV-Stereo [8] on KITTI-2015 [9]. Row 4: Visual comparison with Monster-Stereo [10] on Booster. Our models outperform previous methods, especially in ill-posed regions (white boxes).

Fig. 9. Zero-Shot qualitative results on our captured real-world data. Our GREATEN-DepthAny-IGEV outperforms other iterative methods, where ”DA” stands for ”DepthAny”. All the models are trained exclusively on Synthetic datasets.

Fig. 10. In-Domain qualitative results on SceneFlow [24] test set. Our GREATEN-IGEV outperforms other iterative methods in occluded (Row 1), textureless (Row 2), and repetitive texture (Row 3) regions.

TABLE II SYN-TO-REAL ZERO-SHOT GENERALIZATION ON NON-OCCLUDED ETH3D AND MIDDLEBURY. UNLESS SPECIFIED, ALL MODELS ARE TRAINED ON SCENEFLOW [24]. ”DA” REPRESENTS ”DEPTHANY”. * DENOTES TRAINING ON OUR SYN-TO-REAL MIXED DATASETS.
